_note: Sidecar for the cruise-seasickness article. Health content for a vulnerable audience — every medical claim is grounded in the CDC Yellow Book (Motion Sickness), verified in-session 2026-06-18, not written from training memory. NO affiliate links: per admin/W4-FTC-AFFILIATE-POSTURE.md Section 4 decision tree, health/medical-adjacent content is a permanent affiliate bright line (motion-sickness products explicitly named). Products are named generically as guidance, never linked for commission.
verification_session: All-audience seasickness guide build 2026-06-18. Author lived anchor (Quantum-class Pacific/Tasman crossing, comfortable) carried from the Anthem voyage-pack voice-audit attestation.

affiliate_posture.rule: admin/W4-FTC-AFFILIATE-POSTURE.md Section 4: 'If health/medical-adjacent: stop. Permanent bright line.' plus Section 2.1 naming motion-sickness patches, Sea-Bands, sleep aids, anti-nausea wristbands. A prior Sea-Bands affiliate link was removed under this rule.
affiliate_posture.decision: Operator chose 'Seasickness, no links' (2026-06-18). Products (ginger, wristbands, OTC antihistamines, scopolamine patch) named generically as guidance only; zero affiliate or sponsored links. Internal links point to free site tools (Stateroom Check, deck-plan guide) and to the travel-insurance article.

medical_claims._source: U.S. CDC Yellow Book — Motion Sickness: https://www.cdc.gov/yellow-book/hcp/travel-air-sea/motion-sickness.html (lead WebFetch + corroborating WebSearch of the CDC Yellow Book 2026 edition, 2026-06-18)

habituation_most_effective.claim: Natural adaptation (habituation) is the most effective countermeasure, more than any medication
habituation_most_effective.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'Habituation is the most effective countermeasure—even more than any medication—but can be slow to acquire')

avoid_bow.claim: Avoid the bow of a ship; midship/low moves least
avoid_bow.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'Avoid locations such as the bow of a ship')

horizon_view.claim: A horizon view is protective but often impossible below deck
horizon_view.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'Obtaining a horizon view' provides protection, 'often not possible (e.g., if below deck at sea)')

avoid_reading.claim: Reading and visual scanning worsen motion sickness
avoid_reading.source: CDC Yellow Book (reading/visual scanning 'shown to reduce motion sickness' when avoided)

alcohol_smoking.claim: Alcohol and smoking increase motion sickness
alcohol_smoking.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'Alcohol consumption and smoking (nicotine) should be avoided because they increase motion sickness')

medication_timing.claim: Medication works best taken before exposure, not after symptom onset
medication_timing.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'Medication is most effective when taken before exposure, rather than after the onset of symptoms')

drug_options.claim: Short trips: meclizine or dimenhydrinate; intense: promethazine; longer (>6h moderate-intense): scopolamine patch
drug_options.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim listing of meclizine/dimenhydrinate, promethazine, and scopolamine patch by exposure)

sedation.claim: The effective antihistamines sedate; non-sedating ones are less effective; scopolamine slightly less sedating than the pills; promethazine most sedating
sedation.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'most common side effect is drowsiness or sedation. Scopolamine is slightly less sedating than dimenhydrinate or meclizine, whereas promethazine is the most sedating') + Yellow Book ('nonsedating antihistamines appear to be less effective')

scopolamine_children.claim: Scopolamine can cause hallucinations and mental confusion in children and should be avoided

trial_dose_home.claim: Try a trial dose at home before departure
trial_dose_home.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'take a trial dose of medication at home before departure to find what works best')

ginger_acupressure_weak.claim: Evidence for ginger/supplements is weak and contradictory; acupressure bands no more effective than placebo
ginger_acupressure_weak.source: CDC Yellow Book (verbatim: 'only weak and contradictory supporting evidence' for diets/supplements/vitamins; 'acupressure bands or similar treatments are no more effective than placebo')

ship_stabilizers.claim: Large cruise ships carry fin stabilizers that reduce roll, and most itineraries favor sheltered water
ship_stabilizers.source: General naval-architecture fact; consistent with the Anthem voyage-pack voice-audit physics correction (hull + active fin stabilizers + beam + displacement). Framed generally, no specific efficacy figure claimed.

shipboard_medical_billing.claim: Shipboard medical visits are billed and generally not covered by ordinary health insurance
shipboard_medical_billing.source: Well-established cruise-industry fact; framed as the rationale for travel insurance (links to existing cruise-travel-insurance-2026 article). Not a hard numeric claim.

lived_anchor.claim: Author sailed a Quantum-class ship across the Pacific including ~30-foot Tasman seas and remained comfortable
lived_anchor.source: Operator first-hand attestation, recorded in admin/voyage-packs/v0.1.4-rcl-anthem-alaska-7n.factcheck.json voice_audit block. Kept to the attested facts (Quantum-class, Pacific crossing, Tasman ~30ft seas, comfortable); no fabricated detail; explicitly hedged that personal sensitivity varies.

voice_audit.authorship_mode: Service/pastoral health content with one lived-grade anchor (author's Pacific crossing). Vulnerable-audience page: pastoral honesty axis applies. Health disclaimer present per PASTORAL_GUARDRAILS sensitive-domain rule.

machine_tells.notes: Plain copulas dominant; no hype; honest counter-framing on ginger/wristbands rather than selling them.

must_be_present.honest_limitation_acknowledged: Yes — 'That's not a promise you will; sensitivity is personal, and I'm not built to get sick easily'; plus repeated 'ask your doctor'.
must_be_present.pastoral_honesty: Yes — opens by naming the reader's fear, dignity-first, closes with reassurance not pity.
must_be_present.real_numbers_specifics: CDC-sourced specifics (drug names, timing, sedation ranking, child-safety) and the lived 30-foot-seas detail.

voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: Health claims fully CDC-sourced and quoted; no efficacy overclaim (FTC substantiation respected); no affiliate conflict; lived-grade anchor present; honest about weak-evidence remedies. Re-confirm against shipped HTML before commit.
voice_audit.outstanding_drift_notes: If the CDC page wording changes, re-verify quotes at next review.
